title: magnolia acuminata f. aurea
intro: Magnolia acuminata f. aurea, also known as golden magnolia or golden sweetbay, is a variety of the sweetbay magnolia (Magnolia virginiana) that has golden yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bark and leaves of Magnolia acuminata f. aurea have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. In addition to its medicinal properties, the plant has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to protect against chronic diseases and promote overall health and well-being.
